context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements edge-side personalization using the shell-caching pattern with Cloudflare's cache API, sets appropriate Vary headers to prevent cache pollution, avoids large library dependencies, and ensures edge writes are idempotent.

checklist.name: caches.default usage
checklist.description: Uses `caches.default` (the Cloudflare Cache API) to store and retrieve the page shell, not a KV namespace or in-memory Map

checklist.name: waitUntil for cache put
checklist.description: Cache writes are done via `ctx.waitUntil(cache.put(...))` or `env.ctx?.waitUntil(cache.put(...))` (background, non-blocking)

checklist.name: Impersonal cache key
checklist.description: The cache key used for `cache.match()` and `cache.put()` is based only on the URL (NOT including customer ID, cookies, or other personalization data)

checklist.name: Vary header on personalized responses
checklist.description: Personalized responses include a `Vary` header set to the customer identifier header (e.g. `Vary: x-customer-id` or equivalent)

checklist.name: No broad Vary: Cookie
checklist.description: Does NOT set `Vary: Cookie` broadly on all responses (only sets Vary on the personalization-specific header that differentiates variants)

checklist.name: Placeholder injection pattern
checklist.description: Personalization is injected by replacing a placeholder string in the cached HTML shell (e.g. `html.replace('<!--CUSTOMER_TIER-->', ...)`) rather than fetching a unique page from origin per customer

checklist.name: Anonymous path returns cached shell
checklist.description: Requests without a customer identifier (unauthenticated users) are served the cached shell directly without triggering any personalization logic

checklist.name: No large library imports
checklist.description: Does NOT import lodash, moment, or other large utility libraries; uses native Web APIs (e.g. native string methods, `URL`, `Response`, `Request`) throughout

checklist.name: Idempotent edge writes
checklist.description: Any write operations (analytics events, cookie setting) are safe to execute more than once — either by design or with an explicit guard — to handle Worker retry scenarios

checklist.name: Prefs from KV not external fetch
checklist.description: Customer personalization data (tier, preferences) is read from an edge KV store, NOT via an inline synchronous fetch to an external API
